publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2023-03-16 23:19:06 UTC.

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Cornell University Chimes Collection, #43-12-433. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Correspondence relating to the renovation of the bells, 1905-1907; brochures, articles, plans, photographs and other miscellany relating to the Chimes; also a broadside announcing an ice cream social marking the beginning of the 1998-1999 renovation; three photograph albums of the project; two cassette tapes of the tuning acceptance tests at the foundry of Meeks, Watson, and Company, Georgetown, Ohio, April, 1999; and program from the World Carillon Federation bulletin, 1999 recognizing the project. Also includes sheet music from Chimes concerts.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Microfilm of ca. 620 pieces of chimes music, 1981; clipping of Cornell Chimes concert advertisement from the Cornell Daily Sun, ca. 1965; Also included: The Cornell Chimes by Clara G. Cornell, 1926; Index to Cornell Chime Music with bookplate "Mrs Willard Fiske"; Song book, miscellaneous photographs; exhibition labels and texts.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Includes records concerning the chimes restoration, 1990-1999; correspondence, 1946-2008; and documents concerning the Cornell Chimes Museum.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Chimesmaster recordings on compact disks: The Cornell Chimesmasters in Concert, Spring 2000; Remembered Chimes, 1998; Remembered Chimes II, Solo recordings, spring 1998; and Remembered Chimes III, n.d. Phonograph record: "The Cornell University Chimesmasters Present The Cornell Chimes, A Stereophonic Collection of Campus Favorites, performed in the Spring, 1979," with program notes and biographical information on the performers on the container.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Cornell Chimes Living History recorded July 2010 (DVD) and video recording of alumni chimemasters from the classes of 1949-2013.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Visitor log books from McGraw Tower from 1940-1945; 1964-1969; 1971-1979; 1991-2017.

userestrict.head: Terms Governing Use and Reproduction
userestrict.p: Due to the fragility and potential degradation of moving image and sound recordings, viewing and listening is limited to items that have been digitized. If an item is in another media format, you may request to have the item digitized for access. Information on ordering access copies may be found on the Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections webpage.
Audio visual media that has been digitized: CD-4955 to CD-4957, and DVD-3331, V-11290 to V-11292.

custodhist.head: Custodial History
custodhist.p:
The following collections have been combined with this one. Chimes Music, #140\mf.64 (Esther Koblenz, Collector) Chimes Miscellany, #43-12-m.291
Cornell University Chimes Music, #14-20-1134
Chimes Music Notebooks, #37-5-2595.(Richard Henry Lee, Collector.)
Cornell University Chimes Miscellany, #43-12-1755. (Lawrence M. Kerecman, Collector)
McGraw Tower (Cornell University). Guestbook, #43-12-1955.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Chiefly correspondence and other records on the manufacture and refurbishment of the bells.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: James F. Akright was an instrument maker at Lewis and Hitchcock Organ Co., Silver Springs, MD.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 1908 photograph of bells.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Payne was Assistant to the Vice President - Business.

did.unittitle: CAC (Chimes Advisory Council) meeting minutes and agendas
Chimes, CU Music Dept. correspondence (xerox copies)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains photographs, newsclippings, and correspondence.

did.unittitle: Chimes refurbishment: Meeks and Watson correspondence and contracts
Chimes refurbishment: final PAR and other tower items
Chimes refurbishment: commencement and reunion 12 bell chime
Correspondence 1946/49 re: converting chime to carillon

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Chiefly correspondence and press releases.

did.unittitle: Library tower chimes (correspondence with Meneely & Co.)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Folder includes Ezra Cornell's 1869 edict on chimes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Folder includes brochure addressed to A. D. White.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: The Meneely Bell Company casted the tower's nine original bells in 1868. The foundry would cast additional bells for Cornell until they closed in 1952.

did.unittitle: Programs from two New York recitals played on Meneely chimes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Texts on Cornell's chimes, including an essay written by A.D.White.

did.unittitle: Bells and Chimes with Particular Reference to Those at Cornell University by Harold Eaton Riegger

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Riegger (AB 1910) served as chimesmaster from 1909-1913.

custodhist.head: Custodial History
custodhist.p: One copy was originally part of collection Chimes miscellany, 43-12-m.291.

custodhist.p: Hardcover copy originally a part of collection Cornell University chimes music, 14-20-1134.

custodhist.p: Donated by Feldman. Originally part of collection Chimes miscellany, 14-20-m.738 (43-12-m.291).
